forecasts.reasoning: The $3.1 million payment on April 9, 2025, looks like a token gesture, very likely timed just ahead of the mid-April IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings to demonstrate good faith in debt restructuring/compensation commitments. Given that it took nearly 5 years since the 2020 Global Compensation Deed to make this first payment, and Zimbabwe's severe liquidity constraints, follow-up disbursements are highly unlikely to happen within the short ~7-week window before June 1, 2025. Unless this was specifically the first of a scheduled monthly payout plan (which is uncommon for such settlements), the next payment would likely be scheduled for a subsequent quarter, half-year, or closer to the IMF Annual Meetings in October. Therefore, the probability of another payment prior to June 1 is low.

Yellow fever is a vaccine-preventable disease. The CDC rarely issues Level 3 (Avoid Nonessential Travel) travel health notices for such diseases, as travelers can reliably protect themselves by getting vaccinated prior to travel. During past massive yellow fever outbreaks (e.g., Brazil 2017-2018, Nigeria), the CDC only issued Level 2 notices. The requirement that the notice must be 'greater than 2' on or after June 1, 2025, makes this even less likely. Therefore, the probability of Colombia reaching Level 3 or 4 strictly for yellow fever is very low.

Dan Osborn was reported to be exploring a 2026 Senate run on April 3, 2025. Typically, an exploratory phase lasts anywhere from a few weeks to several months. Candidates often prefer to formally announce in May in order to build a strong initial fundraising quarter (Q2 ends in June). However, it is also very common for candidates to wait until the fall (e.g., September) of the pre-election year to officially kick off their campaigns, avoiding the slow summer months. Since there are about 8 weeks between the 'exploring' news and the June 1 cutoff, the likelihood that he pulls the trigger early to capitalize on his 2024 momentum and Q2 fundraising is moderate but not guaranteed. I estimate the probability at around 42%, balancing the incentive for an early launch against the possibility of waiting until later in 2025.
